description: Tactic types for intellectual evolution analysis.

values.name: Conceptual Recycling
values.use_when: an earlier concept is reused with continuity across works.

values.name: Strategic Ambiguity
values.use_when: the author preserves productive vagueness to span positions or audiences.

values.name: Audience Bridging
values.use_when: the same idea is reformulated to address a new audience or discourse.

values.name: Vocabulary Migration
values.use_when: terms move across domains while the concept partially persists.

values.name: Methodological Shift
values.use_when: the handling of an inherited idea changes because the analytical method changes.

values.name: Position Reversal
values.use_when: the author later turns against or inverts an earlier commitment.

values.name: Scope Expansion
values.use_when: an earlier claim is widened into a broader theoretical or historical argument.

values.name: Narrative Reframing
values.use_when: the same material is reorganized into a different interpretive story.

values.name: Authority Leveraging
values.use_when: citations or prior positions are used to stabilize a new move.

values.name: Selective Citation
values.use_when: some inheritance is foregrounded while inconvenient antecedents are muted.

guidance: Choose the tactic type that best names the operative maneuver, not just the topic under discussion.
If multiple tactics are present, assign the dominant one to `tactic_type` and explain the rest in `assessment`.
